findings.title: Spread block duplicated verbatim; silent drift risk if logic diverges
findings.why_it_matters: The identical 18-line try/except spread block is copy-pasted into both the n==0 branch and the n<SLIPPAGE_MIN_FILLS branch. The only difference is the ModeledSlippage fields. If the formula or guard logic is ever updated in one branch, the other silently stays stale.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract a helper _spread_derived_bps(symbol) -> float | None that returns the floored half_spread_bps or None on failure. Each branch calls it and builds its own ModeledSlippage.
evidence: Lines 128-144 and 152-168 in the diff are identical except for ModeledSlippage constructor arguments.
The formula (ask - bid) / (2 * mid) * 10_000 and the bid > 0 and ask > bid guard appear in both blocks without abstraction.

findings.title: IBKR get_latest_quote blocks sync route handler up to 90 seconds
findings.why_it_matters: When IBKR is the active broker, get_latest_quote calls self._run(_quote()) which uses run_coroutine_threadsafe + future.result(timeout=30) with 3 retries and 1+2+4s backoff. decide_modeled_bps is called from the sync GET /api/slippage/{symbol} route. The endpoint was previously a fast journal-file read (~1ms). With IBKR configured but market data unavailable, the HTTP request hangs for up to ~90 seconds, blocking one threadpool worker and timing out HTTP clients.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short per-attempt timeout (3-5 seconds) for the spread quote call inside decide_modeled_bps, or cap the _run retries to 1 for this non-critical path by accepting an optional timeout/retries parameter.
evidence: IBKRTradingProvider._run() at broker.py:357: timeout=30.0, retries=3 with exponential backoff 1+2+4 seconds.
IBKRTradingProvider.get_latest_quote() at broker.py:665: calls self._run(_quote()) where _quote() includes await asyncio.sleep(1) for market data settlement.
routes/slippage.py:14: def get_slippage(symbol) is a sync FastAPI route calling decide_modeled_bps() directly.
Previously decide_modeled_bps only read the journal JSON file; network call is a new latency class introduced by this PR.

residual_risks: The except Exception: pass in both spread blocks swallows unexpected errors (e.g., wrong tuple shape from get_latest_quote causing ValueError on unpacking). Silent degradation to 2 bps with no observability.
Alpaca get_latest_quote (broker.py:248-256) has no NaN guard. NaN bid/ask from the API: NaN > 0 is False in Python so bid > 0 catches it safely, but this is untested.

No upper cap on half_spread_bps. For illiquid names with 100+ bps spread, max(2.0, 100.0) = 100 bps modeled slippage per leg. Technically correct per the conservative floor policy but may surprise users in backtest results.
testing_gaps: No test covers the spread-derived happy path in decide_modeled_bps (valid bid/ask returned by a mock provider).
No test covers the floor behavior (spread < 2 bps should clamp to 2.0).
No test covers the n==0 spread fallback when broker raises HTTPException (should fall through to source=default).
No test covers the n<SLIPPAGE_MIN_FILLS spread path verifying measured_bps and fill_bias_bps are correctly propagated into the spread-derived ModeledSlippage.
No test for zero bid/ask guard (bid=0, ask=0) bypassing the spread block.
No regression test verifying existing tests still pass when the lazy from broker import get_trading_provider is attempted and fails gracefully.
